mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits as easier financial conditions, lifting equity multiples and risk appetite. When the policy rate is falling, S&P 500 forward returns over the next month are positive because discount-rate relief dominates slower growth concerns.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS is falling but SPX 20d forward returns are negative in more than 60% of such months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth. That shock lowers expected corporate revenues and margins, so S&P 500 returns over the next month turn negative after the unemployment rate crosses higher.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE acceleration events are followed by positive SPX 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign of the signal is unstable across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and near-term revenue growth for listed firms. When retail sales accelerate, S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive because demand momentum supports earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises by at least 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not improve subsequent SPX 20d returns relative to non-triggered months, or if conditional hit rate remains below 50% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in INDPRO combined with stable or falling CPIAUCSL reflects real output acceleration without an inflation squeeze. That mix signals stronger profit volume and lower real-rate pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if high INDPRO growth accompanied by flat or falling CPIAUCSL does not precede positive SPX 60d returns, or if the joint signal adds no incremental predictive power over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL acceleration is met with a higher FEDFUNDS rate, the real discount rate and policy-tightening risk both rise. This combination compresses valuation multiples and makes S&P 500 returns over the next month negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above 2.5% year over year while FEDFUNDS is above 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-plus-tight-rate episodes are not followed by below-baseline SPX 20d returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant while each series alone remains predictive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals housing-cycle expansion, which feeds construction spending, household wealth effects, and downstream durable demand. That breadth of demand support should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month housing-start streaks are not followed by positive SPX 60d returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears after controlling for general demand growth.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies an authentic labor-market deterioration, not just measurement noise. This joint pattern implies weaker wage income and consumption, so S&P 500 returns over the next month should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS decline plus UNRATE rise does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative SPX 20d returns, or if the joint signal is positive in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDPC1 growth impulse after prior weakness marks a re-acceleration in aggregate demand, which improves earnings expectations and supports equity prices. The signal is strongest over a quarter and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns when GDP growth is re-accelerating.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive after at least one prior quarterly deceleration
failure_condition: Reject if GDP re-acceleration events are not followed by positive SPX 60d returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal is erased by modest revisions to quarterly GDP.
